data.title: Michael Seibel Transitions to Honorary Partner Role at Y Combinator
data.description: According to Odaily, Michael Seibel announced on Wednesday evening via X that he will transition to an 'Honorary Partner' role at Y Combinator after more than 12 years with the organization. Seibel initially joined Y Combinator as a founder, participating twice in the program—first with the streaming startup Justin.TV and later with the streaming app Socialcam. Over the years, he has served as a group partner, managing director, and CEO of the YC accelerator. Recently, Seibel had already stepped back to a less operationally involved group partner role. In a blog post last year, he mentioned his intention to 'hand over my leadership responsibilities,' which would take effect after the completion of the accelerator's winter startup batch.

data.title: Crypto Market Funding Rates Indicate Neutral Sentiment
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data from Coinglass reveals that the funding rates across major centralized and decentralized exchanges indicate a neutral sentiment in the cryptocurrency market. The market is no longer bearish but has not turned bullish either. Specific funding rates for mainstream cryptocurrencies are available, although not detailed here.

Funding rates are a mechanism used by cryptocurrency trading platforms to maintain the balance between contract prices and the prices of underlying assets, typically applied to perpetual contracts. This rate facilitates the exchange of funds between long and short traders, without the platform charging a fee. It adjusts the cost or profit of holding contracts to keep the contract price close to the asset price.

A funding rate of 0.01% signifies a baseline rate. When the rate exceeds 0.01%, it indicates a general bullish sentiment in the market. Conversely, a rate below 0.005% suggests a bearish outlook.

data.title: Michael Seibel to hand over leadership responsibilities at Y Combinator
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Michael Seibel announced at X on Wednesday night that he would move to an "honorary partner" role after working at Y Combinator (YC) for more than 12 years. Seibel initially joined YC as a founder and participated in the project twice-once for the streaming startup Justin.TV and the other time for the streaming app Socialcam. Subsequently, Seibel served as group partner, managing director and CEO of the YC accelerator. It is worth noting that Seibel has recently retreated to a less operational group partner role. In a blog post on YC last year, he wrote that he was ready to "hand over my leadership responsibilities" and "officially take effect" after the accelerator's winter batch of startups is completed. (Techcrunch)

data.title: U.S. Trade Policy May Influence Dollar More Than Monetary Policy
data.description:
According to Odaily, economists from HSBC Global Research suggest that the trajectory of the U.S. dollar may be more influenced by changes in U.S. trade policy rather than monetary policy. In a recent report, they noted that the Federal Open Market Committee's (FOMC) stance in March was sufficiently neutral, not challenging the prevailing bearish sentiment towards the dollar, especially amid declining U.S. Treasury yields. However, they also indicated that this neutrality is not enough to trigger a sustained sell-off of the dollar.

The economists highlighted that after a strong start for U.S. Treasuries this year, the Federal Reserve has maintained a cautious approach. This has led them to adopt a more conservative view on short-term yields, while keeping their forecast for the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield at 3.50% by the end of 2025 unchanged.

Additionally, they pointed out the potential for further corrections in the U.S. stock market, as some indicators from the bank suggest oversold conditions.
